title: phegopteris perforata
intro: Phegopteris perforata, also known as the perforate lady fern or variegated wood fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Phegopteris perforata are numerous, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
